source_jobs.title: Engineering & Product Unsolicited Application (Berlin, hybrid)
source_jobs.description:
A little about us

 - AI-native design: End-to-end workflows where a single prompt produces product shoots, social video, labels and full campaigns without switching tools

 - Millions of users: Built a large user base since 2022, with a clear product bet on where design is heading next

 - Diverse team: 120+ people from 30+ countries, building one product together

 - Product-led: Engineers, product managers, and designers shape what Kittl becomes

 - Strong backing: Over $45M raised from investors who've also backed Slack, Dropbox, and Figma
   
   

Learn more: www.kittl.com/career http://www.kittl.com/career



Please note: we review each application with care, but due to volume, we’ll only get in touch if there’s a relevant opportunity, thank you for understanding.



Your role at Kittl

At Kittl, we’re excited to meet creative minds who share our passion for innovation, design, and collaboration. Even if you don’t see the perfect role listed right now, we’d love to hear from you. Submit your application and let’s explore how you can be part of shaping the future of graphic design together with us!



Areas of Interest

In your application, let us know which of these areas best matches your skills and what you’re most excited to work on at Kittl:

 - Engineering
   
   - Frontend Engineering
   
   - Backend Engineering
   
   - Full Stack Engineering
   
   - DevOps / Infrastructure
   
   - Data Engineering / Data Science
   
   - AI / Machine Learning Engineering
   
   - Engineering Leadership

 - Product & Design
   
   - Product Management
   
   - Product Design
   
   - Product Leadership
     
     

We are looking for someone

 - Exceptionally driven to drive impact and challenge the status quo

 - Who takes extreme ownership & gets things done

 - Who goes above and beyond in their role

 - Who is deeply passionate about what they do



Benefits

 - Maximise your impact: No matter if you’re leading a team or you stand out by your domain expertise - all we care about is supporting you to maximise your own impact

 - Hackathons: Our quarterly hackathons provide an environment to experiment with new concepts, push boundaries, and potentially deliver the next big thing.

 - Kittl Week: Each year, our global team gathers together for a whole week, to work, celebrate, get inspired, and have fun

 - Flexible working hours: Our core hours are 11am–5pm CET, leaving the rest of your schedule flexible to fit your style

 - Remote work: Work up to 50 days (10 weeks) fully remote per year from anywhere in the world, as long as you maintain our core hours

 - Learning & development: Our L&D budget supports your professional growth

 - Mobility benefit: We fully cover your monthly BVG public transport ticket

 - Health and fitness: Urban Sports Club membership discount

 - Vacation: Up to 30 vacation days per year



🌈 At Kittl, we embrace diversity and value every team member's unique background, identity, and experience. We're all about respect, honesty, and inclusivity. Together, we create a safe and supportive work environment where everyone thrives. Join us on this exciting journey of making our company and product even better!

source_jobs.title: Senior DevOps Engineer (Berlin, hybrid)
source_jobs.description:
A little about us

 - AI-native design: End-to-end workflows where a single prompt produces product shoots, social video, labels and full campaigns without switching tools

 - Millions of users: Built a large user base since 2022, with a clear product bet on where design is heading next

 - Diverse team: 120+ people from 30+ countries, building one product together

 - Product-led: Engineers, product managers, and designers shape what Kittl becomes

 - Strong backing: Over $45M raised from investors who've also backed Slack, Dropbox, and Figma
   
   

Learn more: www.kittl.com/career http://www.kittl.com/career



Your role at Kittl

As a Senior DevOps Engineer on Kittl’s Platform team, you will help build and evolve the reliable, secure, and cost-efficient foundation that enables our product teams to ship quickly in an AI-driven environment. You will work across our cloud infrastructure (AWS/GCP), Kubernetes (EKS), CI/CD pipelines, observability, and platform security to improve release confidence and operational excellence.

In this role, you will partner closely with engineers across backend, frontend, data, and AI teams to remove operational bottlenecks, improve developer workflows, and standardize how services are built, deployed, and operated. This is a hands-on role with clear impact, directly contributing to outcomes such as more reliable CI pipelines, faster incident detection, and improved infrastructure cost visibility.



What you'll do

 - Improve CI reliability & developer productivity: Reduce flaky pipelines and shorten feedback loops across CI/CD systems to improve developer experience and delivery speed

 - Strengthen observability & incident readiness: Build actionable dashboards, alerts, and SLOs for key user journeys to help teams detect, diagnose, and resolve issues faster

 - Operate and evolve our Kubernetes platform: Manage and improve our AWS EKS environment, ensuring safe deployments, runtime reliability, and scalable infrastructure

 - Drive infrastructure automation: Expand infrastructure-as-code practices using Pulumi and TypeScript; build reusable platform primitives and self-service patterns for engineering teams

 - Improve security & governance: Strengthen access management, secrets handling, and secure defaults across infrastructure and engineering workflows

 - Optimize infrastructure costs: Identify major cost drivers such as data transfer, compute usage, and logging; improve cost visibility and ownership across teams



What you’ll need

 - Kubernetes & cloud expertise: At least 5 years production experience running Kubernetes on AWS (EKS) and troubleshooting distributed systems

 - CI/CD expertise: Solid experience with CI/CD systems (GitHub Actions or similar) and a track record of improving pipeline reliability

 - Infrastructure as Code: Experience with Pulumi, Terraform, or similar tools and building reusable infrastructure patterns

 - Observability knowledge: Practical experience with metrics, logging, tracing, and monitoring tools such as Grafana

 - Security mindset: Understanding of IAM, access control, secrets management (e.g., Vault), and secure operational practices

 - Collaborative approach: Comfortable working cross-functionally and driving technical initiatives from ambiguity to measurable outcomes



Interview process

 - Recruiter interview (30 min)

 - Technical interview I (60 min)

 - Technical interview II (60 min)

 - Interview with CTO (30 min) & cross-functional team interview (30 min)

 - Bar raiser interview with Head of Data (45 min)



Our core tech stack

AWS, Kubernetes, Pulumi, Docker, Argo CD, GitHub Actions, Prometheus, Grafana, PostgreSQL, Redis, TypeScript/Node.js



We are looking for someone

 - Exceptionally driven to drive impact and challenge the status quo

 - Who takes extreme ownership & gets things done

 - Who goes above and beyond in their role

 - Who is deeply passionate about what they do



Benefits

 - Maximise your impact: No matter if

source_jobs.title: Head of Engineering (Berlin, hybrid)
source_jobs.description:
A little about us

 - AI-native design: End-to-end workflows where a single prompt produces product shoots, social video, labels and full campaigns without switching tools

 - Millions of users: Built a large user base since 2022, with a clear product bet on where design is heading next

 - Diverse team: 120+ people from 30+ countries, building one product together

 - Product-led: Engineers, product managers, and designers shape what Kittl becomes

 - Strong backing: Over $45M raised from investors who've also backed Slack, Dropbox, and Figma
   
   

Learn more: www.kittl.com/career http://www.kittl.com/career



Your role at Kittl

As Head of Engineering, you’ll lead the people side of our engineering organization.

You'll own people leadership and delivery across these teams, reporting to the CTO. Your reports are a group of engineers plus a couple of team leads. You'll manage the ICs directly, help growing the team leads into stronger leaders, and keep delivery moving across the board.

You'll partner closely with Product and Marketing to manage engineering capacity and make sure what we build lines up with where the company is going.

 

What you'll do

 - Lead the team: Manage the engineers reporting to you directly, and coach your team leads

 - Streamline execution: Audit and improve development processes to eliminate bottlenecks, reduce waste, and increase team predictability and velocity

 - Set people standards: Define consistent and fair approaches to performance, feedback, career growth, and onboarding, while ensuring teams are structured effectively and people are aligned to the highest-impact opportunities

 - Connect up and across: Give leadership clear, regular updates on progress and risks, and translate company strategy into what your teams actually work on

 - Stay technically sharp: Weigh in on architecture and technical direction with your teams, and keep close enough to the code to make good calls and earn trust

 - Support the vision: Work with the CTO to shape and communicate engineering strategy, and help carry it through the org

 

What you’ll need

 - Experience: 8+ years in software engineering, with at least 5+ years managing engineering teams

 - Growing leaders: Experience developing senior engineers and team leads, and a readiness to grow them into managers over time

 - People-first leadership: You get energy from growing people, and you've built teams others want to join

 - Technical depth: Strong background in software development, architecture, and systems design. You can effectively steer technical strategy, uphold engineering standards, and empower teams to own execution

 - Stack: Comfortable with TypeScript and a React / Node.js stack, familiar with modern frameworks

 

Interview process

 - Recruiter interview (45 min)

 - CTO interview (60 min)

 - Product Lead interview (45 min)

 - Meeting the team (back-to-back, 30 min + 30 min)

 - Bar raiser interview with CEO (45 min)

 

Our core tech stack

TypeScript, Python, Node.js, React, Next.js, Postgres, Pulumi, k8s

 

We are looking for someone

 - Who leads with care and still holds a high bar

 - Who takes extreme ownership and gets things done

 - Who's driven to make an impact and challenge the status quo

 - Who focuses on outcomes over output - ensuring the team delivers actual business value, not just lines of code

 

Benefits

 - Maximise your impact: No matter if you’re leading a team or you stand out by your domain expertise - all we care about is supporting you to maximise your own impact

 - Hackathons: Our quarterly hackathons provide an environment to experiment with new concepts, push boundaries, and potentially deliver the next big thing.

 - Kittl Week: Each year, our global team gathers together for a whole week, to work, celebrate, get inspired, and have fun

 - Flexible working hours: Our core hours are 11am–5pm CET, leaving the rest of your schedule flexible to fit your style

 - Remote work: Work up to 50 da
